CCP4i2ReportDiv.style: width:100%;border-width: 1px; border-color: black; clear:both; margin:0px; padding:0px;

CCP4i2ReportGeneric.p: Space group: P 21 21 21
Key statistics for Dataset: P1/xtl1/ds1

CCP4i2ReportGeneric.div:
Resolution of input data: 1.79Å
Estimates from I/sigI > 1.50
- Overall, resolution estimate 1.79Å
- Along h axis, resolution estimate 1.79Å
- Along k axis, resolution estimate 1.79Å
- Along l axis, resolution estimate 1.79Å
In outer bin: Mean(I/sdI) 4.0
Anomalous Q-Q plot slope = 1.230
Possibly significant anomalous signal

CCP4i2ReportGeneric.p: No automatic resolution cutoff was requested

b.font: ✔ No evidence of possible translational non-crystallographic symmetry
● Warning: Some anisotropy detected. This may have an effect on statistics.
✗ Warning: Severe deviation from Wilson plot, 3.2% of bins deviate, please look at it

CCP4i2ReportDiv.style: width:100%;border-width: 1px; border-color: black; clear:both; margin:0px; padding:0px;

CCP4i2ReportGeneric.p: Data internal consistency statistics
Summary of statistics for merged dataset ds1
Note that CC(1/2) is derived from correlating I+ and I-

CCP4i2ReportDiv.style: width:100%; border-width: 1px; border-color: black; clear:both; margin:0px; padding:0px;

CCP4i2ReportDiv.CCP4i2ReportText: Analysis as a function of resolution

ns0:ccp4_data.title: Analysis against resolution, ds1
ns0:ccp4_data.headers: N 1/d^2 Dmid Rmrg Rfull Rcum Rmeas Rpim Nmeas AvI RMSdev sd I/RMS Mn(I/sd) FrcBias Chi^2 Chi^2c

plot.title: I/sigma, Mean Mn(I)/sd(Mn(I))
plot.description: I/sigma = I/RMS = I/(rms scatter before merging). Mean(I/sd) after averaging, ~= signal/noise

plot.title: Filtered Mean(Chi^2)(<5sd) v Resolution
plot.description: Goodness of fit Mean(chi^2) should be close to 1.0

plot.title: Mean(Chi^2), Mean(Chi^2)(<5sd) v Resolution
plot.description: Goodness of fit Mean(chi^2) should be close to 1.0

plot.title: Rmerge, Rfull, Rmeas, Rpim v Resolution
plot.description: Rmerge, classic R-factor; Rfull, for fully-recordeds only; Rmeas, multiplicity-weighted R; Rpim, precision-indicating R

plot.title: Average I, RMSdeviation and Sd
plot.description: RMSdev is RMS scatter, sd is average corrected sig(I) estimate

plot.title: Fractional bias
plot.description: FrcBias = Mean( Mn(If) - Ip )/Mean( Mn(I) ), where If is a full if present, else the partials with the smallest number of parts

ns0:ccp4_data.title: Anisotropy analysis of CC(1/2) and I/sd, ds1
ns0:ccp4_data.headers: N 1/d^2 Dmid CC:a* CC:b* CC:c* I/sd:a* I/sd:b* I/sd:c* CCp1 CCp2 CCp3 CCft:a* CCft:b* CCft:c*

plot.title: Anisotropic CC(1/2) v resolution
plot.description: CC(1/2) in cones along principle directions. Estimated maximum resolution limits, a*: 1.79, b*: 1.79, c*: 1.79

plot.title: Anisotropic Mn(I/sd) v resolution
plot.description: Mn(I/sd) in cones along principle directions

plot.title: Projected CC(1/2) v resolution
plot.description: CC(1/2) projected on principle directions

ns0:ccp4_data.title: Wilson plot
ns0:ccp4_data.headers: 1/resol^2 ln(I/I_th) Reference_prot

CCP4i2ReportDiv.style: width:100%;border-width: 1px; border-color: black; clear:both; margin:0px; padding:0px;

CCP4i2ReportDiv.CCP4i2ReportText: Analysis for twinning from ctruncate, more details in Istats section
This dataset is probably NOT twinned

ns0:ccp4_data.title: L-test for twinning
ns0:ccp4_data.headers: |L| N(L) Untwinned Twinned

plot.title: cumulative distribution function for |L|, twin fraction of 0.04

CCP4i2ReportGeneric.p: Values for these data, and for ideal data (untwinned or twinned)

CCP4i2ReportDiv.style: border-width: 1px; border-color: black; clear:both; margin:0px; padding:0px;

CCP4i2ReportGeneric.p: Lattice group name P 2 2 2

Accept flags: ">", accepted; "=" original accepted; "-" original rejected
Dcell: maximum cell difference in degrees

CCP4i2ReportFold.label: Systematic absence analysis for each zone

CCP4i2ReportGeneric.p: Systematic absence analysis for each zone, Laue group P m m m
'Missing' means data for this zone are systematically absent, may have been removed previously

ns0:ccp4_data.title: Axial reflections, axis a (lattice frame) screw axis 2(1)
ns0:ccp4_data.headers: Index I sigI I/sigI I'/sigI

plot.title: I/sigI vs. index, axis a, screw axis 2(1)
I vs. index, axis a, screw axis 2(1)

ns0:ccp4_data.title: Axial reflections, axis b (lattice frame) screw axis 2(1)
ns0:ccp4_data.headers: Index I sigI I/sigI I'/sigI

plot.title: I/sigI vs. index, axis b, screw axis 2(1)
I vs. index, axis b, screw axis 2(1)

ns0:ccp4_data.title: Axial reflections, axis c (lattice frame) screw axis 2(1)
ns0:ccp4_data.headers: Index I sigI I/sigI I'/sigI

plot.title: I/sigI vs. index, axis c, screw axis 2(1)
I vs. index, axis c, screw axis 2(1)

CCP4i2ReportGeneric.p: Selecting space group P 21 21 21 as there is a single space group with the highest score

'Reindex' is the operator to convert from the input hklin frame to the standard spacegroup frame.
'TotalProb' is a total probability estimate (unnormalised)
'SysAbsProb' is an estimate of the probability of the space group from observed systematic absences.
'Conditions' are the reflection conditions (absences)

CCP4i2ReportDiv.style: border-width: 1px; border-color: black; clear:both; margin:0px; padding:0px;

ns0:ccp4_data.title: Completeness & multiplicity v. resolution, ds1
ns0:ccp4_data.headers: N 1/d^2 Dmid Nmeas Nref Ncent %poss C%poss Mlplct AnoCmp AnoFrc AnoMlt

plot.title: Completeness v Resolution
plot.description: %poss, completeness in shell; C%poss, cumulative completeness. Anomalous completeness (AnomCmpl) is the percentage of possible anomalous differences measured. AnomFrc is the % of measured acentric reflections for which an anomalous difference has been measured

plot.title: Multiplicity v Resolution
plot.description: Total multiplicity and anomalous multiplicity

ns0:ccp4_data.title: === Scales v rotation range, ds1
ns0:ccp4_data.headers: N Run Phi Batch Mn(k) 0k Number Bfactor Bdecay

plot.title: Mn(k) & 0k (theta=0) v. batch
plot.description: Mn(k) (red) is the mean scale over the whole resolution range for each Batch. 0k is the scale at the lowest resolution, ie excluding the relative B-factor

plot.title: Relative Bfactor & Decay v. batch
plot.description: The relative B-factor is largely a radiation damage correction. Negative values below perhaps -10 may indicate sever radiation damage. Bdecay is a straight-line fit to the B-factors

ns0:ccp4_data.title: Analysis against all Batches for all runs, ds1
ns0:ccp4_data.headers: N Batch Mn(I) RMSdev I/rms Rmerge Number Nrej Cm%poss AnoCmp MaxRes CMlplc Chi^2 Chi^2c SmRmerge SmMaxRes

plot.title: Rmerge v Batch for all runs
plot.description: Increase of Rmerge towards the end of a run probably indicates radiation damage. The red line is smoothed over adjacent batches

plot.title: Filtered Mean(Chi^2)(<5sd) v Batch
plot.description: Goodness of fit Mean(chi^2) should be ~1.0 over all ranges

plot.title: Mean(Chi^2), Mean(Chi^2)(<5sd) v Batch
plot.description: Goodness of fit Mean(chi^2) should be ~1.0 over all ranges

plot.title: Cumulative %completeness & Anom%cmpl v Batch
plot.description: In conjunction with radiation damage indicators, cumulative completeness may indicate a suitable point for cutting back poor data. The blue line is the completeness of anomalous differences

plot.title: Maximum resolution limit, I/sigma > 1.0
plot.description: The resolution limit estimate is the point at which I/sig(I) falls below 1.0, red line smoothed over adjacent batches. A sharp increase probably indicates radiation damage

plot.title: Imean & RMS Scatter
plot.description: Major deviations may indicate bad images

plot.title: Imean/RMS scatter
plot.description: Major deviations may indicate bad images

ns0:ccp4_data.title: Correlations CC(1/2) within dataset, ds1
ns0:ccp4_data.headers: N 1/d^2 Dmid CCanom Nanom RCRanom CC1/2 NCC1/2 CC1/2v Rsplit CCfit

plot.title: CC(1/2) v resolution, max resolution 1.79, anom 0.00
plot.description: Resolution estimate: All scores are above the threshold 0.30 ie data extends to the maximum resolution of 1.79A Anomalous resolution: Insufficient data to determine resolution limit

plot.title: RMS anomalous correlation ratio
plot.description: RMS correlation ratio > 1.0 indicates significant anomalous differences

plot.title: Rsplit
plot.description: Rsplit is the R-factor equivalent of CC(1/2), but less useful

ns0:ccp4_data.title: Anisotropy analysis of CC(1/2) and I/sd, ds1
ns0:ccp4_data.headers: N 1/d^2 Dmid CC:a* CC:b* CC:c* I/sd:a* I/sd:b* I/sd:c* CCp1 CCp2 CCp3 CCft:a* CCft:b* CCft:c*

plot.title: Anisotropic CC(1/2) v resolution
plot.description: CC(1/2) in cones along principle directions. Estimated maximum resolution limits, a*: 1.79, b*: 1.79, c*: 1.79

plot.title: Anisotropic Mn(I/sd) v resolution
plot.description: Mn(I/sd) in cones along principle directions

plot.title: Projected CC(1/2) v resolution
plot.description: CC(1/2) projected on principle directions

ns0:ccp4_data.title: Cumulative CC(1/2) in resolution ranges vs. Batch

plot.title: Cumulative CC(1/2)
plot.description: Cumulative CC(1/2) in coarse resolution ranges

ns0:ccp4_data.title: Analysis against resolution, ds1
ns0:ccp4_data.headers: N 1/d^2 Dmid Rmrg Rfull Rcum Rmeas Rpim Nmeas AvI RMSdev sd I/RMS Mn(I/sd) FrcBias Chi^2 Chi^2c

plot.title: I/sigma, Mean Mn(I)/sd(Mn(I))
plot.description: I/sigma = I/RMS = I/(rms scatter before merging). Mean(I/sd) after averaging, ~= signal/noise

plot.title: Filtered Mean(Chi^2)(<5sd) v Resolution
plot.description: Goodness of fit Mean(chi^2) should be close to 1.0

plot.title: Mean(Chi^2), Mean(Chi^2)(<5sd) v Resolution
plot.description: Goodness of fit Mean(chi^2) should be close to 1.0

plot.title: Rmerge, Rfull, Rmeas, Rpim v Resolution
plot.description: Rmerge, classic R-factor; Rfull, for fully-recordeds only; Rmeas, multiplicity-weighted R; Rpim, precision-indicating R

plot.title: Average I, RMSdeviation and Sd
plot.description: RMSdev is RMS scatter, sd is average corrected sig(I) estimate

plot.title: Fractional bias
plot.description: FrcBias = Mean( Mn(If) - Ip )/Mean( Mn(I) ), where If is a full if present, else the partials with the smallest number of parts

ns0:ccp4_data.title: Analysis against resolution, with & without anomalous (Ov), ds1
ns0:ccp4_data.headers: N 1/d^2 Dmid Rmrg RmrgOv Rcum RcumOv Rmeas RmeasOv Rpim RpimOv Nmeas

plot.title: Rmerge, Rmeas, Rpim v Resolution
plot.description: within I+/I- sets, and over all data (Ov values)

ns0:ccp4_data.title: Analysis against intensity, ds1
ns0:ccp4_data.headers: Imax Rmrg Rcum Rmeas Rpim Nmeas AvI RMSdev sd I/RMS Mn(I/sd) FrcBias

plot.title: Rmerge v Intensity
plot.description: The important values are in the top bin: Rmerge: 0.025 Rmeas: 0.036 Rpim: 0.025

ns0:ccp4_data.title: Run 1, standard deviation v. Intensity, ds1
ns0:ccp4_data.headers: Range Mn(I) NF MnF SdF ChiSqF NFc MnFc SdFc ChiSqFc

plot.title: Sigma(scatter/SD), Mn(Chi^2), within 5 sd
plot.description: Sigma(scatter)/SD and Mn(Chi^2) should be close to 1.0 in all intensity bins. If not, then the SD correction has not worked well

plot.title: Sigma(scatter/SD), Mn(Chi^2), within 5 sd, all and within 5 sd
plot.description: Sigma(scatter)/SD and Mn(Chi^2) should be close to 1.0 in all intensity bins. If not, then the SD correction has not worked well

CCP4i2ReportGeneric.p: Scatter plots etc

ns0:ccp4_data.title: Normal probability plot
ns0:ccp4_data.headers: X Y

ns0:ccp4_data.title: Anomalous differences
ns0:ccp4_data.headers: X Y

CCP4i2ReportDiv.style: border-width: 1px; border-color: black; clear:both; margin:0px; padding:0px;

CCP4i2ReportGeneric.p: Resolution estimates

Estimates of limits from CC(1/2) (threshold0.0)
and Mn(I/sd) (threshold 1.50)
estimates extending to the maximum resolution marked '*'
Dataset:P1/xtl1/ds1

CCP4i2ReportDiv.style: border-width: 1px; border-color: black; clear:both; margin:0px; padding:0px;

p.span: Detailed reports on intensity statistics etc

p.font: NO Twinning detected

These tests rely on the fact that it is highly improbably that very weak or very strong reflections will coincide, therefore, the tails for the distribution of twinned datasets will be less pronounced

CCP4i2ReportGeneric.p: Relation between L statistics and twinning fraction: Twinning fraction = 0.000 L statistics = 0.500: Twinning fraction = 0.100 L statistics = 0.440: Twinning fraction = 0.500 L statistics = 0.375:

p.b: Twinning plots based on global statistics

ns0:ccp4_data.title: L-test for twinning
ns0:ccp4_data.headers: |L| N(L) Untwinned Twinned

plot.title: cumulative distribution function for |L|, twin fraction of 0.04

p.font: The Cumulative |L| plot for acentric data, where L = (I1-I2)/(I1+I2). This depends on the local difference in intensities. The difference operators used link to the neighbouring reflections taking into account possible tNCS operators. Note that this estimate is not as reliable as obtained via the H-test or ML Britton test if twin laws are available. However, it is less prone to the effects of anisotropy than the H-test.
Reference: Padilla, Yeates. A statistic for local intensity differences: robustness to anisotropy and pseudo-centering and utility for detecting twinning. Acta Cryst. D59, 1124-30, 2003

ns0:ccp4_data.title: Cumulative intensity distribution
ns0:ccp4_data.headers: Z Acent_theor Acent_twin Acent_obser Cent_theor Cent_obser

p.font: The culmulative intensity, N(Z), plot is diagnostic for both twinning and tNCS. For twinned data there are fewer weak reflections, therefore, N(Z) is sigmoidal for twinned data. However, if both twinning and tNCS are present, the effects may cancel each out. Therefore the results of the L-test and patterson test should be consulted

CCP4i2ReportGeneric.p: Acentric reflections
Centric reflections

p.font: The acentric moments plot can also give an indication of the quality of the high resolution data. Large deviations often indicate bias in the data.

The Completeness max and min resolution indicte the resolution extremes where obs/sig(obs) > N accounts for more than 85 % of observations.
The Completeness with I/sigma above 3 indicates a strong signal, here it is from 34.00 to 2.14A. (The CC1/2 on unmerged intensities gives, perhaps, the best estimate of the useable data.)
This will be used in the analysis of the data.

ns0:ccp4_data.title: intensity completeness vs resolution
ns0:ccp4_data.headers: 1/resol^2 completeness I/sigI>15 I/sigI>10 I/sigI>5 I/sigI>3 I/sigI>2 I/sigI>1 I/sigI>1 Rstandard

plot.title: Resolution plots for completeness at various I/sigmaI levels
Resolution plots for completeness and Rstandard

p.font: The completeness at various resolution limit plots gives the completeness after applying a I/sigI cutoff. The profiles give an indication of the quality of the data. The Rstandard plot (<sigF>/ <F>) gives an alternative indicator. Strongly recorded resolution bins would typically have values below 0.1

Low completeness at low resolution can lead to map distortions and other difficulties. This often arises through experimental effects such as incorrectly alligned crystal, poorly positioned backstop, or over exposure.
Estimate of overall wilson B-factor: 16.31 A^(-2)
Estimate wilson scale factor: 1.22

The isotropic Wilson temperature estimate (B-value) is an approximation to the fall-off of scattering with resolution. This should be correlated with the refined atomic B-values. This averages out any anisotropy in the experimental observations. This approximation will be misleading for strongly anisotropic data.

ns0:ccp4_data.title: Wilson plot
ns0:ccp4_data.headers: 1/resol^2 ln(I/I_th) Reference_prot

p.font: The wilson plot shows the fall off of the mean intensity with resolution. This is then used calculate an absolute scale and temperature factor for a set of observed intensities, using the theory of A C Wilson. The reference_plot is based upon an analysis of high resolution datasets in the PDB (BEST or RNA equivalent), which takes into account the none random distribution of atoms within the crystal. Some deviation from the reference plot is to be expected, however, significant deviation may indicate problems, such as ice rings, detector issues, or missprocessed.
Popov and Bourenkov, Acta D (2003) D59, 1145

CCP4i2ReportGeneric.p: Possible bad resolution rings found, consisting of 3.2% of bins. Data in these may be of lower quality.

p.font: The outlier rings table shows data Z-scores and completeness for problem resolution bins in comparison with the Wilson B-factor fit. Large z-scores and low completeness at give a strong hint to the presence of problems. It may be required to exclude these resolution ranges.
No translational NCS detected.

The analysis uses the peak heights in the patterson map that are further than 14 A (approx. 4 Ca-Ca) from the origin. The presence of a large off origin peak (above 20%) and/or a very low Q-score, below 1.0, is a string indicator of the presence of tNCS. An intermidiate Q-score, between 5.0 and 1.0, may indicate weak tNCS or be the result of cross vector of a large scatterer such as a cluster or heavy metal.
Reference: P. Zwarts CCP4 Newsletter 42
Some anomalous signal at intermediate resolution. Low resolution signal missing.

These calculations are performed using scaled and merged data. More accurate estimates of the limit of the anomalous signal can be obtained using scaled and unmerged data in the half dataset correlation calculation of aimless.
The measurability is defined as the fraction of the anomalous differences for which the signal to noise for deltaI, I(+) and I(-) are all > 3. The resolution limits are derived from I/sigI of 1.2 for the signal to noise, and measurability above 5% For well processed data having more than 5% of the measured data satisfying this criteria is a good indicator, particularly when combined with a significate mean signal to noise of the anomalous difference in a resolution shell.

ns0:ccp4_data.title: intensity anomalous analysis plots
ns0:ccp4_data.headers: 1/resol^2 Mn(dI/sigdI)) Mn(dI/I) measurability

plot.title: Resolution plots for anomalous scattering indicators

p.font: Little or no anisotropy detected. The presence of anisotropy may indicate that the crystal is poorly ordered along one of the axes.

p.b: Eigenvalues and Eigenvectors of anisotropy matrix

ns0:ccp4_data.title: Intensities Anisotropy Analysis
ns0:ccp4_data.headers: 1/resol^2 Mn(I(d1)) Mn(I(d2)) Mn(I(d3)) Mn(I(ov) Mn(I/sd(d1)) Mn(I/sd(d2)) Mn(I/sd(d3)) Mn(I/sd(ov)) N(d1) N(d2) N(d3) N

plot.title: Directorional plots for number of reflections

p.font: The directional plots are along the directions of the moments of the anisotropy temperature matrix. These are ordered such that d1 has maximum alignment with a*, d2 with b*, etc.

CCP4i2ReportGeneric.p: This will be used in the remaining statistics calculations.
Possible ice rings found. Data in these may be of lower quality.
Ice rings will be ignored in the truncate procedure.

p.font: The ice rings table shows data Z-scores and completeness for ice ring sensitive resolutions in comparison with neighbouring ice-ring insensitive resolutions. Large z-scores and low completeness at give a strong hint to the presence of ice rings. It may be required to exclude these resolution ranges.

CCP4i2ReportReference.articleTitle: How good are my data and what is the resolution?
CCP4i2ReportReference.source: Acta Crystallogr D Biol Crystallogr. 2013 Jul;69(Pt 7):1204-14. doi:

CCP4i2ReportReference.articleTitle: An introduction to data reduction: space-group determination, scaling and
CCP4i2ReportReference.source: Acta Crystallogr D Biol Crystallogr. 2011 Apr;67(Pt 4):282-92. doi:
